question.content: In triangle ABC, AB=AC. Point D lies on side BC but does not coincide with either endpoint, and segment AD is drawn. Rotate segment AD counterclockwise about point A by angle alpha to obtain segment AE, and connect DE. In Figure 1, alpha=angle BAC=60 degrees and angle CAE=20 degrees. Find angle ADB. Answer format requirement: <expr>. Strictly forbidden: any extra text beyond this format.

step_1.content: Since AB=AC and angle BAC=60 degrees, triangle ABC is equilateral, so angle ABC=60 degrees.
step_1.key_point: Convert the isosceles triangle with a 60-degree apex into an equilateral triangle.

step_3.content: The rotation sends AD to AE through 60 degrees. With angle CAE=20 degrees and AC lying between AD and AE, angle DAC=60-20=40, so angle BAD=60-40=20.
step_3.key_point: Use the rotation angle and the 20-degree split at A to recover angle BAD.

step_5.content: Because D lies on BC, angle ABD equals angle ABC=60 degrees. Then in triangle ABD, angle ADB=180-20-60=100.
step_5.key_point: Finish with the angle sum in triangle ABD.
